steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes a specific gap: filtration can't capture very small microplastics. The test results show the sound wave system removes particles down to 10 microns — the size range filtration misses. The correct inference connects those two facts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "unlike sound waves, filters have the ability to remove large microplastics from polluted waterways.": reverses the relevant contrast. The passage says filters struggle with very small microplastics, not that sound waves cannot remove large ones. ✗
  • "the sound wave treatment system may help solve a problem associated with filtration of microplastics.": the system removes what filtration can't — this directly bridges the passage's problem and the test result. ✓
  • "directing water through filtration tubes with multiple outlets reduces microplastic pollution.": misstates the setup; the device uses a metal tube and sound waves, not filtration tubes. ✗
  • "combining filtration with sound waves is likely the most efficient method for removing microplastics from water.": goes beyond the evidence. The passage never tests efficiency or a combined method. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
